This market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the listed candidates who advance from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with a party.

In case any combination of parties advances that is not listed here, including all scenarios where an independent candidate advances, this market will resolve to "Other".

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Reza Pahlavi in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US crude oil inventories dropped 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels for the week ending April 24, per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, driven by robust refinery inputs averaging 16.1 million barrels per day and elevated exports amid steady US production near record highs. Traders monitor weekly EIA releases—next on May 6—for continued draws, influenced by global oil demand forecasts slowed to 0.6 million barrels per day growth in 2026 per EIA's STEO, OPEC+ output cuts, and potential SPR releases. Resolution hinges on the June 4 report covering the week ending June 3, barring adjustments.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (LOW) $116 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Sinaloa Gov. Ruben Rocha extradited to US by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Rubén Rocha Moya is extradited to the United States between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Extradited to the United States” means Rocha Moya is physically transferred to U.S. government custody pursuant to an extradition process or equivalent custody transfer process. If Rocha Moya otherwise enters U.S. territory and is subsequently arrested or taken into U.S. government custody by the market’s end date, this will also qualify.

An announcement that extradition has been requested, approved, ordered, or agreed to will not qualify unless Rocha Moya is physically transferred to, arrested by, or otherwise taken into U.S. government custody by the market’s end date.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Rubén Rocha Moya, the governments of Mexico and Sinaloa, the United States government, and relevant courts; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US Department of Justice indicted Sinaloa Governor Rubén Rocha Moya and nine other officials on April 29 for allegedly conspiring with the Sinaloa Cartel's Chapitos faction to traffic fentanyl, heroin, cocaine, and methamphetamine into the US in exchange for bribes and political support. The US promptly requested extradition, but President Claudia Sheinbaum insisted on overwhelming evidence within 60 days per the bilateral treaty, as Mexico opened its own probe. Rocha, proclaiming innocence, requested temporary leave from office on May 2 to aid the process, heightening diplomatic friction. Traders monitor evidence submission deadlines and Mexico's track record of shielding high-profile figures amid cartel pressures.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid leads trader consensus at 44% implied probability for the Hart Memorial Trophy after clinching his sixth Art Ross Trophy with a league-high 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists) in 82 games, capping a late-season surge that propelled the Oilers into playoff contention while posting a point in all 41 team wins. Nathan MacKinnon's 20% share reflects his Maurice "Rocket" Richard Trophy-winning 53 goals and elite two-way play driving the Avalanche's strong regular season, despite finishing third in points. Nikita Kucherov at 15.3% benefits from 130 points (44 goals, 86 assists) that carried the Lightning to the postseason, highlighted by strong voter support in recent trackers. Macklin Celebrini's 5.9% acknowledges his historic rookie output—fourth in league scoring—elevating the last-place Sharks, though team success tempers his MVP case amid a tight race defined by regular-season dominance.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 20-39 Truth Social posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This keeps mid-ranges viable but elevates extremes due to historical late-night barrages exceeding 150 posts. Separation could arise from next week's schedule, breaking news on policy or foreign affairs like Iran, or quiet executive time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026 parliamentary elections, which yielded no outright majority, incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement conceded failure to form a coalition on April 20 after a month of talks, notifying President Nataša Pirc Musar. The president declined to nominate a candidate on April 25 amid stalled negotiations, advancing the process to a second round where parliamentary groups propose PM-designates for National Assembly confidence votes starting May 6. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), the election runner-up, drives trader consensus with center-right bloc momentum—including proposals to trim ministries—positioning him as the frontrunner, while alternatives like Jernej Vrtovec trail amid uncertain coalition math.

markets.question: Will Reform UK win control of the most London borough councils?
markets.description:
Local elections in London, UK are currently scheduled for May 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the party that controls the most London borough councils as a result of this election.

Control is defined as having more than half of the voting councillors of a London borough council. Any executive positions, like Mayor, will not count.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of councils controlled by the specified party, not any coalition of which it may be a part. 

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of councils controlled, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose name comes first in alphabetical order, as listed in this market group.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant London borough council elections, once those results are official. This market may resolve to the listed party as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of councils controlled in these elections. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 London local elections aren’t known by April 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 London local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the respective council government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ahead of the May 7 local elections across London's 32 borough councils, a YouGov MRP poll released April 22—based on late March to mid-April fieldwork—drives trader consensus, projecting Labour to secure the highest vote share and likely control in 15 boroughs, down six from 2022 but well ahead of rivals. Labour's capital-wide vote has fallen 16 points to 26% amid national government dissatisfaction, with Greens surging to 22% (+10 points) in inner strongholds like Hackney, Lambeth, Lewisham, and Waltham Forest, positioning them as the primary challenger. Half the boroughs feature races within five points, risking no overall control, while Conservatives (17%) and Reform UK (14%) trail due to fragmented right-wing support in outer areas. Liberal Democrats hold steady at 15%.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Senator Lindsey Graham dominates trader consensus at 86.5% implied probability to win South Carolina's Republican Senate primary on June 9, driven by his commanding fundraising lead—over $20 million raised and $11 million cash on hand versus Mark Lynch's $5.7 million—along with endorsements from President Trump, Governor Henry McMaster, and Senator Tim Scott, bolstering his incumbency advantage in the deep-red state. Challenger Mark Lynch holds 12.4% after Paul Dans, architect of Project 2025, dropped out on April 10 and endorsed him, consolidating some anti-Graham sentiment amid polls showing Graham at 41% to Lynch's 21% (March Pulse survey). Fragmented opposition and Graham's establishment support keep upset odds low despite criticism over foreign policy stances, with a potential runoff on June 23 if no candidate exceeds 50%.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, with Yulia Navalnaya edging out Donald Trump (7.5% vs. 6.5%) and Volodymyr Zelenskyy (6.4%) amid the Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 disclosure of 287 secret nominations—208 individuals, 79 organizations—sparking fresh speculation without revealing frontrunners. Navalnaya's slim lead stems from her vocal anti-Putin activism carrying forward Alexei Navalny's dissident legacy, while Trump's momentum builds on claims of brokering an Israel-Hamas ceasefire and prior nominations from world leaders; Zelenskyy's bid highlights Ukraine's prolonged resistance to invasion. Lower odds for mediators like Qatar's Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani (3.9%) and peace advocate Pope Leo XIV (3.8%) underscore competitive dynamics favoring high-profile defiance over diplomacy, with resolution looming October 9 amid unpredictable committee preferences for humanitarian underdogs.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 20°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Central Weather Administration's latest forecast for Taipei shows a May 5 high of 18–22°C under persistent cloud cover with occasional showers or thunderstorms, driving trader consensus toward outcomes around 22–24°C and well below the monthly mean of 29.7°C. This cooler outlook stems from an early Mei-yu front ushering in moisture-laden southerly flow, reducing solar insolation via high cloud albedo and limiting daytime heating—a hallmark of Taiwan's pre-monsoon transition. Close probabilities between 22°C, 23°C, and 24°C reflect uncertainty in partial clearing timing, urban heat island effects at the official station, and short-range model spreads of ±2°C; watch CWA updates through May 4 for refinements as observations firm up resolution criteria.

Kathy Hochul's April 13 endorsement—following Michael Bloomberg's earlier backing—and strong fundraising in the wide-open June 23 race. Assemblymember Alex Bores holds at 33% after his campaign's April 17 poll showed gains among informed voters, while Jack Schlossberg's 21.5% reflects Kennedy family name recognition from March-April surveys. The crowded 18-candidate field fragments votes, keeping the top trio tight amid recent forums at 92nd Street Y and ACS; upcoming debates, senior-heavy turnout, or additional endorsements like labor unions could consolidate support and tip the balance.

As a three-term lawmaker and former floor leader, Park benefits from DPK dominance in Incheon party ID surveys and voter preference for aligned governance in this key metropolitan battleground. Yoo, seeking a third term as People Power Party nominee, trails despite incumbency advantages. Upsets could arise from scandals, gaffes, late PPP surges, or turnout shifts favoring conservatives before ballots close.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jimmy Lai's February 2026 sentencing to 20 years in prison by Hong Kong's High Court for conspiracy to collude with foreign forces and sedition under the national security law anchors trader consensus at 97.5% against his release by June 30. The 78-year-old pro-democracy publisher opted not to appeal the conviction in March, closing major procedural avenues amid Beijing-aligned judicial enforcement. April developments, including government bids to seize over $16 million in Lai's assets, reinforce the finality of his incarceration. Absent extraordinary factors like severe health deterioration prompting compassionate release or unlikely diplomatic interventions, no scheduled hearings or legal shifts signal any path to freedom within the resolution window.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 16°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 87% implied probability to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 of 81 seats (two per state) will be contested amid opposition momentum. PL recently solidified its position as the Senate's largest bloc with 15 seats after January defections from PSD, bolstering incumbency advantages and candidate recruitment. State-level polls, such as AtlasIntel in São Paulo and Paraná Pesquisas elsewhere, show PL contenders like Carol de Toni and Carlos Bolsonaro leading in battlegrounds like Santa Catarina, while a JOTA analysis last week highlighted opposition edges in most states. Flávio Bolsonaro's competitive presidential polls further energize PL's base, though coalition shifts or Lula-aligned surges could narrow the gap before candidate filings and primaries.

markets.question: Will Trump post "Free Tina Peters" on Truth Social this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if @realDonaldTrump posts/truths the listed term between April 27, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, all text posted by the listed account in quote and reply posts/truths count toward a "Yes" resolution, but quoted posts/truths and reposts/reTruths will not count.

Text posted in images, memes, or other non-animated, non-video media that are not strictly text will qualify towards a "Yes" resolution only if the listed term is spelled out clearly and in full. (e.g., words spelled out in a letter posted as a .jpg will qualify, however a word posted as part of an animated .gif will not.)

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: r@d1cal, for "radical") will disqualify it from counting toward a "Yes" resolution. Sigils at the beginnings of words, such as hashtags, "@" symbols, or denotations of currency, will not disqualify a term from counting towards a "Yes" resolution.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

The resolution source for this market will be Donald Trumps's verified Truth Social account: @realDonaldTrump

Please note, only the @realDonaldTrump verified Truth Social account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Donald Trump posts/truths from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will SpaceX IPO by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If SpaceX is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

Alexander Zverev trails at 8.1% as the top clay specialist, leveraging strong Foro Italico history and recent wins over Sinner. markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price an 81.5% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the FOMC's April 28-29 decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75%—the third consecutive pause—despite March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year on an energy surge. This stance aligns with the March dot plot's median projection of 3.4% by year-end, signaling ample restriction amid 4.3% unemployment and solid growth. Hawkish risks from hotter April CPI data, due May 12, or renewed inflation pressures linger but face high hurdles given recent cuts and Chair Powell's final emphasis on data dependence ahead of his May 15 departure. Next FOMC in June looms as a key pivot.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Shadow Secretary" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister's Questions occurs every Wednesday at noon in the House of Commons when Parliament is sitting, with the next session scheduled for May 6 following the April 29 clash where Keir Starmer defended his administration against Kemi Badenoch's attacks on economy, energy policy, and welfare reforms. Trader consensus hinges on opposition grilling points drawn from fresh developments, including escalating Iran tensions, US President Trump's tariff threats involving Greenland, and Starmer's recent pledges for accelerated defence spending amid national security concerns. Starmer's weekend interviews emphasized closer EU economic ties and crisis comparisons to 2008 and Brexit, prompting 'Brexit betrayal' claims, while Speaker Lindsay Hoyle urged No. 10 to stay on-topic. Markets track verbatim mentions of these high-salience terms in Starmer's responses.

markets.question: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid, entrenched in fourth place in the La Liga table after 33 matches, host sixth-placed RC Celta de Vigo on May 9 at Riyadh Air Metropolitano in a pivotal late-season clash for Champions League qualification. Los Colchoneros' recent form includes gritty home wins over Athletic Bilbao (3-2) and Getafe (1-0), bolstered by strong head-to-head dominance (19 wins in 30 meetings), though injuries to José Giménez (muscle), Dávid Hancko (ankle), and Pablo Barrios (thigh) temper squad depth. Celta, winless in their last five with a 1-0 loss to Barcelona, face away form woes despite a 1-1 draw at home earlier this season; key absences like Manu Fernández (muscle) add pressure amid their Europa League push. Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Will voter turnout be less than 85% in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the official voter turnout rate for the 2026 Maltese general election, defined as the total number of votes cast divided by the total number of registered voters.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market will resolve based on the official results as reported by the Government of Malta, including the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices self-funded businessman Perry Johnson as the frontrunner at 57% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, ahead of U.S. Rep. John James at 37.5%, driven by Johnson's heavy ad spending and name recognition from his 2022 statewide bid despite past ballot troubles. Recent developments include Johnson's April 22 lawsuit against James over campaign ads referencing the 2022 signature fraud rejection, escalating their feud, and James drawing rival ire for skipping a key debate last week to attend congressional votes. While polls like the Detroit Regional Chamber's late-April survey show James leading 37%-20%, traders appear to discount them amid Johnson's financial edge and petition filings now facing validation challenges. Lower-tier candidates trail far behind in a crowded field.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 10°C or below on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 17°C (31%) and 18°C (30.5%) for Seoul's highest temperature on May 4, reflecting inherent uncertainty in short-range forecasts amid persistent cloudy skies and a recent cool air mass. Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) observations confirmed a midday peak of just 15°C on May 3, capped by extensive cloud cover limiting solar insolation and light westerly winds hindering boundary layer mixing—well below early May climatological averages of 20–23°C. While KMA-linked projections suggest a modest rebound to 19°C tomorrow, model ensembles like GFS and ECMWF show divergence in afternoon heating potential, with cloud thinning or persistence as key differentiators between 17°C and 18°C outcomes. Watch KMA's final short-term forecast update later today for refined guidance ahead of resolution based on official Seoul station measurements.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, vacated by Gov. Mikie Sherrill, has solidified trader consensus at 95% odds for a 20-25% margin of victory over Republican Joe Hathaway, reflecting post-election tallies with over 99% of votes reported. Late provisional and mail ballots through late April, including a flip of Passaic County's NJ-11 portion to Mejia, boosted her performance by roughly 12 points above Kamala Harris's 2024 district showing amid strong Democratic early voting turnout and suburban overperformance typical in House specials. Pockets of softer support in Jewish-heavy towns like Livingston showed resistance to her progressive stance, but the margin held firm. Scenarios challenging this include a GOP-heavy surge in remaining ballots or certification disputes, though unlikely given current counts ahead of official canvass.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the tight clustering of latest forecast models around a Moscow high of 23–24°C on May 4, driven by a stabilizing high-pressure ridge over European Russia ushering in southerly winds and clear skies for enhanced solar insolation after late-April's cold snap with blizzards and highs below 5°C. The slight edge to 24°C (36.5%) stems from warmer GFS ensemble runs factoring urban heat island effects and maximal afternoon heating, while 23°C (29.5%) aligns with more conservative ECMWF guidance anticipating minor cloud intrusions; 22°C and 25°C trail due to lower model probabilities. Uncertainties persist in boundary-layer mixing and exact wind speeds, with Rosgidromet updates and Vnukovo station observations resolving the market tomorrow. Historical May norms hover near 18°C, underscoring this above-average warmth.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $75 on the final trading day of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver futures (SI) trade near $76 per ounce as of early May 2026, reflecting trader consensus on persistent supply deficits—the second-largest in two decades per Silver Institute data—and surging industrial demand from solar photovoltaics, electric vehicles, and AI hardware, up 9% in recent years. A weaker U.S. dollar and Fed rate cut expectations enhance silver's appeal as an inflation hedge and safe-haven asset, with the gold-silver ratio elevated around 65:1 signaling relative undervaluation. Recent 5% intraday gains on May 1 stemmed from strong Chinese import data amid global economic uncertainty. Key catalysts ahead include May 14 CPI release, June 6 nonfarm payrolls, and June FOMC meeting, where softer inflation could spur further upside toward $80+ forecasts, though renewed dollar strength poses downside risk.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 72.5% implied probability, reflecting congressional gridlock on federal AI safety legislation despite the bipartisan American Leadership in AI Act introduced April 27, 2026, which codifies the AI Safety Institute, expands research resources, and adds whistleblower protections but omits strict prohibitions on AI model creation, training limits, usage restrictions, or mandatory human-in-the-loop requirements needed for market resolution. The Trump administration's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework prioritizes light-touch rules, child safety measures, and state law preemption to foster innovation, clashing with demands for comprehensive safeguards amid partisan divides in the 119th Congress. With bills stalled in committees and states like New York advancing narrower laws, enactment before year-end remains unlikely barring lame-duck breakthroughs.

markets.question: Will Amazon have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This model excels in agentic coding, SWE-Bench, and multimodal benchmarks, widening its edge over Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro (15% odds, trailing at ~1490 Elo) and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 (1.4%, further back). With no confirmed rival launches, stability persists, though Google I/O in late May could introduce surprises challenging Anthropic's frontier positioning.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors no U.S.-Cuba military clash in 2026 at 55.5%, despite President Trump's May 2 remarks in Florida hinting at a U.S. Navy "takeover" of Cuba "almost immediately" post-Iran operations, prompting Cuban condemnation of "unprecedented" threats today. Pentagon contingency planning accelerated in mid-April amid an oil blockade causing Cuban blackouts, yet bilateral talks persist—U.S. delegations visited Havana April 18, Cuba released prisoners as goodwill, and both sides explore diplomatic resolutions. Senate rejection of a resolution curbing military action against Cuba on April 29 adds uncertainty, but absent a direct order, traders weigh rhetoric against de-escalation signals and U.S. focus elsewhere.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 27% implied probability to win the 2026 WNBA championship after re-signing core stars Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu while adding three-time All-Star Satou Sabally in free agency, bolstering their 2024 title-winning depth amid a competitive 15-team field with expansion sides Golden State Valkyries, Toronto Tempo, and Portland Fire. Indiana Fever sit close at 16% on the breakout trio of Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston's new extension, and Kelsey Mitchell's scoring prowess, assuming health sustains their momentum. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces hold 15.5% behind A'ja Wilson's supermax re-signing and dynasty experience, but odds slipped as rivals upgraded; Atlanta Dream's 11% reflects strong offseason grades via key acquisitions, setting a wide-open postseason path influenced by early-season form, injuries, and playoff matchups.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a SpaceX IPO closing market cap above $2.0 trillion at 54% implied probability, driven by the company's confidential SEC filing in early April 2026 targeting a June listing at around $1.75 trillion—recently boosted by secondary market trades hitting $1.48 trillion and hype around explosive Starlink subscriber growth exceeding 10 million alongside Starship's rapid testing cadence toward orbital refueling. Private tender offers have escalated valuations from $800 billion in late 2025 to over $1.25 trillion post-xAI synergies, underscoring SpaceX's dominance in reusable launch vehicles and global satellite broadband. Lower brackets trail due to these momentum signals, with prospectus release expected late May and roadshow in early June as key catalysts; no-IPO odds languish below 4% amid confirmed IPO momentum.

No significant injuries or withdrawals have disrupted the top since Jake Knapp's pre-tournament thumb sprain exit; mild Miami weather favors aggressive play on the long layout, though final-round volatility on exposed greens could allow mid-pack surges for top-10 value.

markets.question: Spread: CA Platense (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 6 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CA Platense" if CA Platense win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Estudiantes de La Plata".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaprofesional.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Platense vs. Estudiantes de La Plata - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 6 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Estudiantes de La Plata hold second place in the Liga Profesional Apertura table with an impressive defensive record, conceding only nine goals across 10 matches, bolstering trader consensus for their away challenge at Platense's Estadio Ciudad de Vicente López on May 3. Platense sit 10th amid a mid-table campaign hampered by injuries to key defender Gonzalo Goñi (Achilles tendon rupture, out until August), Iván Gómez (hamstring), Héctor Bobadilla (bruise), and others like Franco Minerva (strain). Estudiantes also miss Santiago Arzamendia (cruciate ligament tear) and José Sosa (Achilles issues), but their form edges the matchup. The sides' prior March fixture was postponed, following Estudiantes' 2-1 Trofeo de Campeones win in December.

markets.question: Will "Dandelion - Ella Langley" be the Billboard 200 #1 album for the week of May 9?
markets.description:
Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then dated “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”.

This market will resolve according to the number 1 album on the Billboard 200 chart dated “Week of May 9, 2026”.

This market will resolve as soon as the relevant chart is published. If the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week is not published within 14 calendar days of the expected release date, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week, published on the Billboard website (https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/) or through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the U.S. military capture of incumbent President Nicolás Maduro on January 3, 2026, and his transfer to face U.S. charges, Vice President Delcy Rodríguez was sworn in as acting head of state by Venezuela's Supreme Court and recognized by the military, consolidating chavismo control amid absent elections despite her 90-day interim mandate expiring in April. Recent U.S. commercial flight resumption to Caracas on May 1 and oil sector reentry signal economic thaw under Rodríguez's stewardship, yet ongoing repression and military reshuffles sustain trader bets on regime continuity. Maduro leads implied probabilities via disputed UN credentials and constitutional status claims during his trial, while opposition leader María Corina Machado garners support abroad without institutional traction; no election timeline exists ahead of year-end resolution.

markets.question: Putin and Zelenskyy shake hands by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy shake hands by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the handshake to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, there must be video or photographic evidence.

The resolution source for this market will be photographic or digital evidence verified by a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus at 97.3% "No" for Putin and Zelenskyy shaking hands by June 30 reflects entrenched diplomatic standoffs in the Russia-Ukraine war, with no direct leader-level talks scheduled despite recent overtures. Zelenskyy is seeking details on Putin's short-term May 9 Victory Day ceasefire proposal to Trump, while Ukraine pushes for a summit in Turkey or Azerbaijan to revive U.S.-led efforts. The Kremlin conditions any meeting on finalizing a peace deal, citing absent political will from Kyiv amid continued airstrikes and territorial disputes. Absent a sudden de-escalation, major concessions, or late-breaking mediation success, high barriers like mutual distrust and battlefield hostilities sustain near-certain trader expectations of no encounter.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship with 75 points after three Grand Prix wins across Australia (P2), China (P1), and Japan (P1), edging teammate George Russell's 68 points from his Australian victory and consistent podiums, reflecting the squad's dominant race pace and front-row lockouts that have swept every race podium so far. Trader consensus prices Antonelli at 33% and Russell at 28.5% implied probability, bunched tightly due to their intra-team rivalry, shared superior Mercedes machinery, and Antonelli's back-to-back triumphs making him the youngest championship leader despite a Miami sprint penalty dropping him to P6 behind McLaren's 1-2. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc trails third at 55 points, while Red Bull's Max Verstappen and McLaren's Lando Norris lag further amid Mercedes' early-season edge, though a 21-race calendar leaves room for rivals to close via upgrades and consistency.

markets.question: Will JD Gaming qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman's dominant position in solidly Republican Wisconsin's 6th Congressional District underpins trader consensus pricing a GOP hold at 80.5%, reflecting the district's R+8 partisan voting index and history of double-digit Republican margins, including Grothman's 61%-39% 2024 reelection win. With over $700,000 cash on hand through late March, Grothman leads fundraising against a fragmented eight-candidate Democratic primary field—led modestly by Bradley Smith at $53,000—lacking a clear frontrunner ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries. Independent challengers like union firefighter Michael Thurow add competition but face structural barriers in this safe seat rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report, with no recent polling to shift sentiment.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 19°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters tightly around 15–19°C for Seoul's highest temperature on May 5, driven by divergent short-range forecasts from the Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA), which projects a 22°C high under clear skies following May 3–4 rain, contrasted by cooler Incheon Airport guidance at 18°C max and AccuWeather at 15°C with lingering showers. Recent cool, rainy conditions—May 3 high of 16°C amid 60% precipitation probability—have suppressed early May averages below seasonal norms of ~23°C, fostering caution on post-frontal warming. Differentiating factors include southerly wind strength for warm air advection versus potential persistent clouds or northerly residuals, with Seoul's urban heat island possibly boosting readings 1–2°C above rural sites. Next KMA updates and ECMWF/GFS runs within 24 hours will refine model consensus.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 0 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from April 27 - May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between April 27, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: USGS seismic monitoring confirms zero earthquakes of moment magnitude 6.5 or greater worldwide from April 27 through May 2, 2026, per the agency's real-time catalog, driving the market's 92.5% implied probability on zero total events as the period nears resolution on May 3. This aligns with historical baselines of roughly 15–20 such moderate-to-strong quakes annually—about 0.3 per week—where Poisson-distributed seismic activity often yields quiet weeks amid global tectonic strain accumulation on subduction zones and faults. Trader consensus reflects elapsed time without qualifying events (e.g., no M6+ even recorded), but a late-day rupture exceeding Mw 6.5, potential magnitude revision of a borderline quake, or undetected deep event could challenge it before close. Updated USGS feeds expected hourly.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.
